meta.title: fak persona-fit scorecard — across each feature space, how much would each persona like fak?
meta._doc: A persona × feature-space FIT matrix. Each persona carries a WEIGHT vector over the fixed value dimensions (what that person values across any feature); each feature space (rows-*.json) carries a DELIVERY vector grounded against the real tree (a path that exists, a command that resolves, a CLAIMS section that is tagged). The cell fit = weighted match (persona weights · feature delivery). The score cannot be gamed by editing data alone: a delivery claim must name a grounding check that resolves in the tree, and a persona's declared favourite must match the computed argmax. Regenerate the doc: python tools/persona_fit_scorecard.py --markdown-dir docs/persona-fit-scorecard

feature_groups.name: Trust floor
feature_groups.note: the kernel stops believing the model; memory + tools governed

feature_groups.name: Throughput / model
feature_groups.note: the in-kernel model, KV reuse, the serving path

feature_groups.name: Platform / adoption
feature_groups.note: the binary, the gateway, the way a person lands

personas.values: Will download a binary and run it; spends seconds. Cares almost only that something runs RIGHT NOW with no key, no GPU, no reading.
Drops fak in front of an existing agent harness. Wants it to run today, to govern tool calls (trust), to be wired in (extensible), and documented.
Embeds fak into a real service against a frozen interface. Weighs extensibility + a config/ops surface + docs + the trust floor it is buying.
Has to run it in production. Weighs an operable deploy/config surface + metrics to watch (measured) + the trust floor; runs it today.
Is here for the security floor. Weighs trustworthiness above all, then proof it holds and metrics to audit it.
Wants to reproduce and understand. Weighs proof (a witness/results doc), benchmarks vs the field, and being able to run it today.
Lives in the numbers. Weighs benchmark harnesses above all, then proof and the ability to run a sweep today.
A product manager deciding to adopt. Weighs competitive benchmarks, metrics/observability, docs, proof, and the trust story.
Wants to add a leaf without forking. Weighs extension seams above all, then docs, proof (tests), and a fast local run.
A machine that discovers + drives fak. Weighs runnable verbs + machine-readable docs, then extension seams and the trust floor it operates under.
